blockingIssues.description: apply_refactor() method exists at line ~3304 but is never invoked in execute(). The method remains dead code - only simulated by pushing description strings to applied_opportunities. This is the same blocking issue from round 1 that persists through round 4. The feature description requires wiring apply_refactor() into execute().

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add enforcement that cargo clippy must be run before completing validation. Workers should verify with 'cargo clippy --workspace' and fix all warnings before marking a feature complete.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-refactorer-validation worker did not run clippy despite it being a known issue from prior round. All workers should be running clippy as part of validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that 'In a full implementation...' comments without TODO/FIXME tracking are insufficient. Deferred implementation items must have tracked follow-up items, not just comments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-refactorer-apply has comments 'In a full implementation, we would... Apply the refactoring via apply_refactor()' but no tracking mechanism. This is the third round where this pattern appears.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For fix features, explicitly verify the blocking issue described in the original feature is resolved. Do not partially address requirements - verify all specified behaviors are implemented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-refactorer-apply correctly implemented validation before/after and revert logic, but failed to wire apply_refactor() as specifically described in the feature requirements.

rejectedObservations.observation: fix-coder-agent-llm: Worker modified multiple files beyond the assigned feature scope
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking convention observation. The actual implementation is correct and tests pass. Scope creep is a guidance issue, not a blocking code issue.
